description: User states a specific budget figure (£47,500) at turn 0, then 18 user turns of unrelated chat follow, then asks at the end for the figure. Designed to fail under naive-truncate-16 and pass under full-history. Numerical recall is harder than name recall because the model can't lean on word-shape associations.

turns.content: Setting context for a few questions today — I'm scoping a kitchen renovation and the contractor's first quote came in at £47,500. Mention it now so we don't have to rehash later if I bring it up. Mostly random other stuff first though.
Got it — kitchen reno, contractor at £47,500. Noted. What's first?
Actually random — what's a good lightweight backpack for hand-luggage flying, around 35L?
Aer Travel Pack 3 if you want premium, Osprey Farpoint 40 for budget, Peak Design Travel 30L if you want camera-friendly compartments.
Aer noted. Coffee — how do I make oat milk foam properly without a pro machine?
Use barista-edition oat milk (Oatly Barista is standard), heat to 60–65°C, then a handheld milk frother for 20–30 seconds. Standard oat milk won't foam — the barista versions add a stabilizer.
Oatly Barista, helpful. Sleep — magnesium glycinate, worth it?

Mixed evidence. Some people respond well, especially if dietary intake is low. Glycinate form is gentler on the gut than oxide. Try 200–400mg an hour before bed for two weeks; if nothing changes, it's not your bottleneck.
Will try. Movie — if I liked Arrival what should I watch?
*Annihilation* (2018), *Ex Machina*, *Children of Men* if you want the same emotional register. Older: *Solaris* (Tarkovsky version, only if you have patience).
Annihilation, will rewatch. What's a good way to learn touch-typing again as an adult?
Keybr.com adapts to your weak letters; 15 minutes daily for two weeks resets most muscle-memory issues. Don't look at the keyboard — that's the only rule.
Keybr it is. Hiking — a single boot recommendation for muddy UK trails?
Salomon X Ultra 4 GTX for the all-rounder, Scarpa Terra GTX if you want something stiffer. Both grip mud well; both annoy you on hot dry trails.
Salomon, noted. Garden question — easiest herb to grow indoors with low light?
Mint and chives are the most forgiving in low light. Basil wants more light than people give it. Parsley is patient but slow.
Mint then. What's a fair price for a Sunday roast for two in central London?
Mid-range £30–40 per head, premium £50–70. The Camberwell Arms or Blacklock are reliable in that band; Hawksmoor sits at the upper end.
Blacklock, sounds good. Coding — Vim or VSCode for someone returning to programming after years?
VSCode. Vim has higher ceiling but the setup tax is real and the LSP-tooling story is now better in VSCode. You can always graduate to Neovim later if you find VSCode limiting.
VSCode. Wine — a reliable bottle under £15 for a casual dinner?
Côtes du Rhône village wines hit that band well, or a Garnacha from Spain. The Wine Society's house claret is a long-standing safe pick.
Côtes du Rhône, will pick up. What's a quick stretch routine for someone who sits at a desk all day?
Five moves, five minutes: thoracic spine rotation, hip flexor lunge, doorway pec stretch, forward fold, and chin tucks. Daily beats heroic.
Will try. Music — a working playlist that isn't lo-fi-beats slop?
Brian Eno's *Music for Airports*, Nils Frahm's *Spaces*, or any of Hania Rani's recent piano work. All have texture without lyrics pulling attention.
Hania Rani, noted. Clothing — a single pair of jeans worth investing in?
A.P.C. Petit Standard is the perennial pick — raw denim, fades in well, holds up for years. Levi's 501 if you don't want to pay for the brand premium.
A.P.C. Quick — the difference between matcha and ceremonial-grade matcha?

Ceremonial grade is from the youngest spring leaves, stone-ground, meant for whisking with water. Culinary grade is older, coarser, fine for lattes and baking. If you whisk, pay for ceremonial; otherwise culinary is fine.
Ceremonial it is. Bike — what's a reasonable commuter bike for under £700?
Triban RC500 or RC520 (Decathlon) is the budget benchmark. If you want a flat bar, the B'Twin Riverside 500 is fine. Anything from a Halfords Apollo line — skip, you'll regret it within a year.
Triban noted. Thermos — a flask that actually keeps coffee hot for 8+ hours?
Zojirushi SM-SA48 is the gold standard. Stanley Classic if you want something rugged and dishwasher-tolerant. Cheap thermoses lose 30°C in three hours; the Zojirushi loses maybe 5–10.
Zojirushi, will look. Last one — best way to back up a laptop without thinking about it?
Time Machine on macOS to a 2 TB SSD plus Backblaze for off-site. Set both up once and ignore. The hardest part is buying the SSD; the rest is automatic.
OK back to actual planning. What was the contractor quote I mentioned at the start? I want to write it down before I forget it.
